Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

commit.message:
test: cover droid platform renderer contract

Add Droid-specific inherited trait coverage for supporting directories,
dryRun behavior, and custom outputDir handling in the platform renderer
contract suite.

Task: @01KKJKM7Y5M5366F6XWC0KMHV5
Spec: @droid-renderer
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(daemon): handle missing watcher roots cleanly

Attach a Bun fs.watch error handler so deleted project roots do not surface as unhandled ENOENT failures during daemon session-sync teardown. Stop cleanly when the watched .kspec root disappears instead of scheduling recovery retries, and cover the deleted-root path with a focused regression test.

Task: @01KKJP6E1TN5ZFMHASTHSGG2MH
Spec: @dispatch-workspace-cleanup-policy
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: GitHub action 웹훅과 shorebird 빌드 흐름 정리 (#4)

* fix: 로컬 CI/CD 파이프라인 구조와 온보딩 정리

- Python 중심 오케스트레이션과 단계 가시성 구조로 리팩토링
- 대시보드와 진단 화면을 강화하고 설치 진입점을 단순화
- 처음 pull 받은 뒤 따라갈 수 있는 한글 가이드를 추가

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: GitHub 액션 엔드포인트와 웹훅 정책 정리

- GitHub action 경로를 /github-action/* 기준으로 정리
- Shorebird patch를 GitHub 시그니처 검증 흐름으로 통합
- develop -> main 머지 기반 prod 트리거 정책과 테스트 추가

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore: dev 웹훅 브랜치 prefix 규칙 조정

- WEBHOOK_DEV_HEAD_PREFIX 기본값을 release/dev로 변경
- 관련 문서와 템플릿 예시를 현재 규칙에 맞게 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: 웹훅 정책을 원래 운영 규칙으로 복원

- dev는 release/dev* -> develop 머지일 때만 트리거
- prod는 main 기준 태그 생성일 때만 트리거
- 과한 prod PR 머지 규칙과 관련 설정 제거

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore: 레거시 웹훅 엔드포인트 제거

- /webhook alias를 제거하고 GitHub action 경로만 유지
- API 문서와 설정 가이드를 새 경로 기준으로 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore: env.sample 제거

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: dev 빌드 웹훅 기준을 release/dev 머지로 변경

- dev는 release/dev* 브랜치로 머지될 때만 트리거
- 관련 env 키를 WEBHOOK_DEV_BASE_PREFIX로 단순화
- 문서와 테스트를 새 운영 규칙에 맞게 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat: shorebird 태그 트리거와 수동 패치 빌드 추가

- github-action/shorebird는 prod 태그 생성 시에만 빌드 트리거
- /build/shorebird 수동 엔드포인트를 추가해 shorebird patch 빌드 실행 지원
- 관련 문서와 테스트를 새 동작에 맞게 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add droid ACP adapter

Register the built-in droid ACP adapter and cover resolution, listing, and auto-approve behavior.

Spec: @droid-acp-adapter
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(sdk): expand Direct API coverage and align core contracts

Summary:
- add service support for ad images, agency clients, bid modifiers,
  dictionaries, keyword bids, keywords, and sitelinks
- align core Ads, Campaigns, and AdGroups behavior closer to the
  official API surface, including broader Ads payload support
- add explicit request apiVersion support for official v5 and v501
  service endpoints
- extend README and contract tests to cover the expanded SDK surface

Rationale:
- move the SDK from MVP coverage toward broader Yandex Direct API
  coverage while keeping the transport and error model consistent
- remove non-official AdGroups methods and reflect official endpoint
  versioning in the public request options

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Rebuild missing dispatch workspace records from metadata-backed worktrees,
normalize rollout-era legacy worker branches onto canonical dispatch/task/*
branches, and emit per-task queue discard diagnostics.

Task: @01KKJP6E1TN5ZFMHASTHSGG2MH
Spec: @dispatch-workspace-cleanup-policy
Spec: @dispatch-scheduling-priority-model

ci(repo): add GitHub Actions test and release workflows

Summary:
- add a minimal Linux CI workflow for format, compile, and test checks
- add a tag-driven release workflow that validates the tag version,
  builds docs and the Hex package, and creates a draft GitHub release
- update RELEASE.md to describe the new changelog and draft-release
  automation flow

Rationale:
- the repo needs a small default CI path that matches its current
  Linux-first library constraints without app or container complexity
- release automation should follow the documented Hex-library flow and
  fail early when the pushed tag does not match the package version
- the manual release checklist must stay aligned with the automated
  GitHub release draft behavior to avoid process drift

Validation:
- git diff --check
- local workflow files reviewed; GitHub Actions were not executed from
  this session

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve loop guard recovery and runtime reliability

Includes loop warning recovery flow, config + prompt updates, DB schema-init optimization, improved tool annotations, and diagnostics scripts.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(api): clarify root EtherCAT public contracts

Summary:
- add named public types for the root EtherCAT API surface, including
  session state, query errors, and slave/domain snapshots
- tighten root specs to use those aliases instead of loose map and
  list return types
- fix docs for bus nil semantics, state query errors, and structured
  slave configuration_error values

Rationale:
- the root module is the public entry point, so its contracts should be
  explicit and readable without chasing internal implementation files
- state query failures are wrapper-call errors, not lifecycle states,
  and the docs should make that distinction obvious
- inaccurate docs around configuration_error and bus nil behavior make
  the public API easier to misunderstand than it needs to be

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat.ex
- mix compile
- mix docs.fresh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(dispatch): cover workspace and scheduler flow

Add an integration-style dispatch flow test and fix the in-flight queue handoff so follow-up review and fix-cycle work can be requeued immediately after an invocation completes.

Task: @01KKFY9K4NK8JC0ANF9TS7H769
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(sdk): add missing Yandex Direct service methods

Summary:
- add missing Campaigns methods for delete, archive, and unarchive
- add Ads methods for delete, archive, unarchive, and moderate
- add AdGroups delete and expose ads/adGroups on the public client
- update README and tests to cover the expanded SDK surface

Rationale:
- align the implemented SDK methods with the official service method sets
- keep transport behavior and response contracts validated by tests

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs: bootstrap symphony playbook repository

Summary:
- add a README focused on OpenAI Symphony setup notes and field logs
- add the first note covering Linear/Symphony review and rework handling
- add lightweight cases, templates, and bootstrap planning docs

Rationale:
- start with a logbook-first repository that matches the current exploration stage
- preserve the initial workflow question and the code-backed conclusions for future reuse

Tests:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Run bot every 6 hours

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Update workflow actions to current majors

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(simulator): add harder clustered recovery scenarios

Summary:
- add ordered trace assertions and bounded trace formatting to make
  long recovery scenarios fail deterministically
- add scenario 35 for chained mailbox disconnect and SAFEOP recovery
  faults and scenario 36 for captured EL3202 reconnect PREOP failure
- add a hardware-shaped simulator ring helper and tighten existing
  reconnect mix scenarios around causal subsequences

Rationale:
- harder clustered fault stories should exercise realistic hardware-like
  failures without relying on fragile global trace ordering
- limiting trace dumps prevents failing scenarios from timing out while
  formatting unbounded telemetry history
- captured-device coverage closes part of the gap between synthetic
  protocol fixtures and the real lab ring

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/support/expect_test.exs
  test/integration/support/scenario_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/31_reconnect_preop_mailbox_and_disconnect_mix_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/34_reconnect_preop_event_triggered_disconnect_mix_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/35_reconnect_preop_disconnect_safeop_chain_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/36_el3202_reconnect_preop_event_disconnect_mix_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Paginate Linear projects for the team view (#230)

* chore(repo): remove shared symphony workflow files

Summary:
- remove the repo-level WORKFLOW.md used only for local Symphony runs
- remove the copied .codex skills from the shared bug-board repo
- keep the repo clean for contributors who are not using Symphony

Rationale:
- Symphony now uses a machine-local workflow outside the shared repo
- temporary issue workspaces still receive the needed skills during setup
- this avoids leaving local automation files in a shared codebase

Tests:

fix(input): replace numeric values instead of appending

Summary:
- select existing type="number" values in the shared Input on
  focus and mouseup
- skip text caret bookkeeping for numeric inputs that do not expose
  selection offsets

Rationale:
- Chromium reports null selection positions for number inputs, so
  replacement typing appends to the existing value
- native select() behavior restores expected overwrite entry across
  calculator fields and other shared numeric inputs

Tests:
- npm run typecheck
- PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=.codex-tools/ms-playwright timeout 60s
  node .codex-tools/validate-number-input.mjs

commit.message:
fix(dispatch): release stranded in-flight task keys

Clear inFlightTaskKeys when _spawnInvocation fails before an invocation promise is registered, so bootstrap and prompt configuration errors do not strand the task. Add regressions for both early-failure branches.

Task: @01KKFY9HBMSAW4DD924SYTEJ0P
Spec: @dispatch-role-workflow-entry-contract
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

HON-8 Add project-scoped filtering within a session (#24)

* feat: add project-scoped session filtering

* feat(session-view): improve project and codex run visibility

Summary:
- keep the project filter inside the session card and remove the
  redundant duplicate display
- make initial prompt resolution work from full session context even
  when project filtering is active
- improve Codex session ingestion so direct and Symphony-linked runs
  surface more reliably in AgentActa

Rationale:
- the session view was harder to use with duplicated project tags and
  brittle filtering behavior
- Codex activity was happening on disk and in Symphony logs, but not
  consistently showing up in AgentActa where it belongs
- distinguishing direct and Symphony-origin Codex runs makes the
  session list more truthful and useful

Tests:
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(session-view): address project attribution review feedback

Summary:
- attribute delta tool results using prior session tool-call context
- handle Windows absolute paths during project extraction
- add attribution tests for delta context and Windows path cases

Rationale:
- live project-filtered views should not lose attribution when the
  matching tool call was indexed in an earlier batch
- Windows-origin sessions should resolve to the correct project instead
  of falling back to workspace

Tests:
- node --test tests/project-attribution.test.js
- node --test tests/db.test.js tests/config.test.js
- npm test (blocked in sandbox by listen EPERM on network-binding suites)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(session-view): tighten live attribution context

Summary:
- load prior session neighborhood for live delta attribution
- narrow filesystem path detection to real path-like values
- add focused tests for delta context and path heuristics

Rationale:
- live project-filtered views need prior context for new message events,
  not only linked tool results
- slash-containing metadata should not be mistaken for filesystem paths
  and create false workspace attribution

Tests:
- node --test tests/project-attribution.test.js tests/delta-attribution-context.test.js

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs: update session filtering and codex visibility notes

Summary:
- document project-scoped session filtering and initial prompt behavior
- document clearer Codex direct vs Symphony session visibility
- add changelog entry for the current release candidate scope

Rationale:
- the branch shipped more than the original narrow ticket title implied
- release notes and README should match the actual user-facing behavior

Tests:
- not run (docs only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:

Summary:
- add scenario 34 for a retained reconnect PREOP mailbox fault that
  arms a later counted PDO disconnect through
  Scenario.inject_fault_on_event/4
- document the new scenario in the simulator coverage indexes and
  advance the next-scenario note to the longer chained helper-only
  case

Rationale:
- replace the older imperative mid-scenario action with a causal
  telemetry-triggered follow-up fault so the helper API is exercised
  directly
- keep the reconnect PREOP plus counted-disconnect overlap as its own
  regression case before composing the longer chained scenario

  test/integration/simulator/34_reconnect_preop_event_triggered_disconnect_mix_test.exs

- document the simulator delay boundary and make it explicit that
  delayed fault scheduling is not a general latency model
- add cycle-level scenario guidance for the simulator integration
  loop, including combination and promotion rules
- add an authoring checklist and anti-pattern section for new
  simulator regressions

Rationale:
- keep future scenario work deterministic and focused on real
  runtime behavior instead of sleep-driven timing
- make the fixture and fault boundaries easier to apply
  consistently when expanding the simulator suite

Validation:

chore(release): cut 0.3.1

Summary:
- set the project version to 0.3.1 and add the 0.3.1 changelog
  notes
- add a forced docs alias and tighten simulator, domain, and DC
  moduledocs for the current release state
- keep moved hardware scripts out of the default test sweep and
  rename the UDP transport script so mix test stays clean

Rationale:
- ship the current main changes in the 0.3.x line so
  kino_ethercat can consume them without a dependency range bump
- make the release validation path trustworthy by removing hidden
  ExDoc warnings and accidental hardware script execution during
  mix test

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs.fresh
- mix hex.build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Stop documenting consecutive declarations (#348)

Stop carrying declaration-scan state past the first declaration
after an AutoDoc source comment block. This makes declaration
documentation match the documented immediate-adjacency rule
instead of implicitly attaching the same comment block to later
consecutive declarations.

Add a regression test for consecutive declarations and make the
general worksheet fixture spell out the later category
declarations explicitly so it no longer depends on the old buggy
behavior.

AI-assisted with Codex for implementation, test updates, and
changelog entry.

Fixes #169

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(simulator): clarify test fixture and hardware guidance

Summary:
- document that telemetry-triggered helper injections should complete
  before the matching callback returns
- expand the simulator testing guidance with real-hardware positioning,
  scenario granularity, and fixture-tier recommendations
- spell out when to use synthetic fixtures, captured real-device
  fixtures, and hardware runs

Rationale:
- the simulator README should capture the current testing policy instead
  of leaving it implicit in recent code and review discussions
- keeping this guidance next to the simulator design makes future test
  additions more consistent and reduces churn over fixture choice

Validation:
- not run (docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(test): make telemetry-triggered scenario faults synchronous

Summary:
- route telemetry-triggered scenario injections through a per-scenario
  task supervisor instead of a bare spawned process
- wait for the triggered injection to finish before returning from the
  matching telemetry callback and surface timeout or task-exit failures
- add a focused regression test and document the stronger helper
  guarantee in the simulator test README

Rationale:
- the previous spawn-based helper only guaranteed "eventually after the
  event" and left follow-up assertions exposed to process-scheduling
  races
- the current scenarios want causal ordering from the telemetry match to
  the injected fault without promoting master events into simulator-core
  milestones

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/support/scenario_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(dispatch): reevaluate legacy publication mode

Recompute publication mode for reopened pending/in-progress dispatch workspaces from current repository capabilities instead of preserving a legacy manual_merge fallback. Add a regression test covering legacy registry reload in a PR-capable repository.

commit.message:
feat(simulator): add event-triggered fault scenarios

Summary:
- allow nested schedulable simulator faults so milestone and timer
  chains can defer faults until their final step
- add scenario helpers and coverage for telemetry-triggered follow-up
  faults, including the new recovering-entry SAFEOP scenario
- add the captured EL3202 support driver with startup mailbox config
  and writable simulator objects, and widen the startup timeout budget

Rationale:
- the new scenarios need simulator-side chained scheduling plus a small
  test DSL for arming faults from master-observed events
- a realistic EL3202 fixture lets the simulator boot path exercise real
  mailbox startup writes instead of only synthetic drivers
- the startup response-timeout scenario needs more than 2.5s under full
  suite load because it includes a full mailbox timeout before the
  master reports activation failure

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- MIX_ENV=test mix run -e '<EL3202 boot validation>'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(parser): make YAML persistence atomic

Prevent readers from observing partially rewritten YAML files during dispatch workspace registry updates, and tighten the foreground daemon shutdown test wait so full-suite verification is repeatable.

refactor(capture): inline generated driver simulator data

Summary:
- make `gen_driver/2` emit self-contained support driver files
- stop writing separate capture artifacts from `gen_driver/2`
- inline capture-derived `definition_options/1` into generated
  simulator modules

Rationale:
- keep generated drivers editable in one place
- reserve `write_capture/2` for explicit raw snapshot output
- avoid extra artifacts and external file dependencies in generated
  drivers

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/capture.ex test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(capture): scaffold integration support drivers

Summary:
- add `EtherCAT.Capture.gen_driver/2` to generate best-effort
  `EtherCAT.Slave.Driver` modules plus companion simulators
- simplify the interactive capture help to point at `gen_driver/2`
  and switch overwrite options to `force: true`
- fix generated capture-backed scaffolds to store relative capture
  paths and cover sibling output directories in tests

Rationale:
- make live hardware capture useful for building integration support
  drivers with a single command
- keep generated scaffolds reviewable while preserving captured
  structure for simulator fallback
- avoid absolute machine-specific capture paths in generated modules

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/capture.ex test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: install-juliaup
repository.description: GitHub Action that installs Juliaup (and uses Juliaup to install Julia)

commit.message:
fix(master): replace stale reconnect fault reasons

Summary:
- keep runtime_faults and slave_faults aligned when a recovering PDO
  slave reconnects, fails its reconnect-time PREOP rebuild, and
  later heals
- add a master regression plus simulator scenario 33 with a
  mailbox-plus-process-data test driver that reproduces the
  stale disconnect fault case
- document the bugfix in the changelog and the scenario note

Rationale:
- a stale {:down, :disconnected} runtime fault could outlive a
  newer PREOP configuration failure and keep the master stuck
  in :recovering after the slave returned to :op
- recovery retries need to replace and clear both tracked fault
  views together so master state matches the live bus state

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/33_critical_pdo_reconnect_preop_self_heal_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
